svg.title: Query Cache Incremental Merge: Merge-on-Write Delete Bitmap Window
svg.desc: A merge-on-write UNIQUE table qualifies for incremental merge per tablet by inspecting the delete bitmap of the delta window. Entries stamped inside the window that target baseline rowsets mean history was rewritten and force one full recompute; entries targeting the delta itself or stamped outside the window are harmless.
svg.text: a stale entry at v100 wants to merge the delta (v100, v101]: may the window be merged on top of the cached blocks?
baseline rowsets: already folded into the cached entry
delete bitmap entries, key = (RowsetId, SegmentId, Version):
stamped v101, targets a baseline rowset
a backfill upsert replaced an old row
stamped v101, targets the delta rowset
the same new key written twice, a losing
sequence-column row, a local delete sign
harmless: the delta scan applies the
incremental merge, exactly as safe as on
one full recompute re-bases the entry at v101;
the next pure-append hour is incremental again
